div1.entryFree: Spite, vb. to thwart malignantly, to mortify: to s. me now, each minute seems a moon, Pilgr. 207. to s. my wife, Err. III, 1, 118. if you s. it (your heart) for my sake, I will s. it for yours, Ado V, 2, 70. that which --s me more than all these wants, Shr. IV, 3, 11. to s. a raven's heart, Tw. V, 134. beguiled, divorced, wronged, --d, slain, Rom. IV, 5, 55. I am reckless what I do to s. the world, Mcb. III, 1, 111.
